You're a great marketer on Facebook and social media. I didn't say he wasn't a good sales trend. I didn't say that. I'm not even going to say that right now. You can make your own judgment. The point was, as I remain that opinion, he's a very good marketer. Remember, marketing and sales are very, very different things. The idea is, as I always say, the secret to success in any business, no matter what you do, you want to have an awesome kick-ass marketing campaign. How do you bring people into your sales funnel? That's a huge thing. Massively important. But there's a certain point when marketing ends and sales begins. What do you do when you get that person on the phone? Well, you're going to find certain things as we learned in the last module in prospecting. What did we learn? We learned that basically when someone comes in your funnel, there's four types of buyers. We'll say there's four types of people, two of which are buyers, two of which are not buyers. We have your buyers in heat and power. These are your potentially good buyers. Those who need, want your product, can benefit from your product and are qualified. It's a very basic concept in sales. Very basic. Then you have those ones that are in heat, meaning there's urgency. They want to buy now. They're feeling pain. It's in the forefront of their mind to make a decision. Then you have those buyers in power, which are the ones that need, want, and could use your product and could afford it. But they're feeling that they are okay. The house isn't on fire, so to speak. They're not as urgent. Still great buyers. Still very much closeable. But that lack of urgency makes them feel like they're in the driver seat. They're in a position of power, hence the term buyers in power. For those of you who want certification, you know I taught you guys a strategy. Again, you're going to actually be revisiting that same strategy now next week, which is script building, which is unbelievable, by the way. You'll be getting that in a few days, the script building module. I'm still adding to it every day, because I never stop with this stuff. But in terms of how do you take a buyer in heat, how do you take a buyer in power and through language and through tonality and body language for that matter, if you're a person, turn that person, elevate that person, and they become a buyer in heat. How do you do that while you're gathering intelligence? Remember, gathering intelligence, prospecting, is not just about identifying who deserves or who is worthy of, who belongs to move down the straight line any further. He said that he goes, because it's not about money. And then he elaborated and he wasn't saying the why is bullshit. It's so obvious. I don't get it. It's not about you. It's not like, unconditionally, your children, a cause. It's really interesting how he cut right to that same truth in a very different way. But the point I'm driving at here is this. How do you know what you should spend your time doing if you don't know where you want to end up? Seriously, how do you tell me how you know what you should be learning? What skills should you be refining? What calls should you be taking? What relationships should you be cultivating? The reason the answer is because I have a vision that's clearly defined target and everything else backs into that. Listen, watch. I'll draw it out for you. As I always say about the straight line system, it's a backwards looking system. Meaning, why does it work so well? Well, there's a lot of reasons. But one thing is, it's caused that moment of clarity that I had back in the late 80s, was like, okay, I'm doing all this stuff automatically without thinking about it. I out close everyone around me by a country mile. It was ridiculous. Well, what's happening is, I turned around and said to myself, well, okay, what has to line up? In my mind, I was, what would I need to say? What would the prospects have to hear in order to feel comfortable and confident enough to say yes? What is it? Backwards being not like, okay, I want to close the guy. That's looking forward. I'm here at the start. I want to close the guy. That's where I'm going, right? But how about a shift in perspective? Imagine I just close the sale, close the deal, achieve some goal in my life, right? You know, accomplish something, right? Well, imagine this. Imagine I've got to do that. How about this? The guy just bought. What happened? What lined up? What were the things that transpired? Why wouldn't he buy in the beginning? What made him buy again? What did I have to line up? You look backwards. Well, I began to realize, well, wait a second. If I, when I got someone to say yes in the past, every time I'd done that, it was because these same key elements got lined up somehow. Those somehow, some way I was able to get somewhere along these three close to two more. I was lining them up by looking backwards. See, I knew where I wanted to go. And by being there, I thought, here's my starting point. It's like vision in reverse essentially, right? Think of it that way. It's like, here's my vision. Okay. I want, I'm starting from my end point. Here I am. Here I am in the future when I got what I want five years down the road where my life is exactly as I want it to be. All things are as they should be in my mind, right? There I am at the start. Here's Jordan five years ago. What did he have to do? What had to change? What things did he have to learn? What organizations and groups did he have to align himself on? What skill sets were required? What do you have to brush up on essentially? What had to happen? And what was the best way? What had to happen? First, second, third? So by starting at the end and I'm like, and I imagine it was done like, wow, look at all the stuff that lined up. That would have never occurred to me, but I had a vision. Now why, why was it you think, let's really dissect this now, why was it that I succeeded, why, how did this happen, think about it, it's the long shot of all long shots for a guy who never wrote a book before, not just to write a best self, but to come out a cult hit around the world, to get the world's most famous actor by far, Leo is, the world's most well respected director by far, he's up there for sure Marty along with James Cameron, Marty and his own, it's nothing like a Scorsese movie, he's a Scorsese, you know, why, how did that happen? Well, let me start by giving you, let me just at least start and finish here by giving you a series of steps and I want to try to put this in a way that I've never ever said it before to you, very different take on why I was able to achieve what I achieved, can you give me a red bull please, there's nothing I'm pretty sure, okay, why I was able to accomplish all that. Step number one, step number one, ready for this? I possessed, I'm going to tell you this is a really interesting thing, I worked on this in a very different way, right? I possessed boatloads of what we call self belief, in other words, in my deepest core, in my crux of who Jordan Belfort is, how Jordan Belfort views himself, what his identity is, I was still a winner in my deepest core, I absolutely had no doubt that I possessed every skill set, ready for this? Every skill set, I possessed them, every strategy, every tactic. I possessed every single one I would ever potentially need to achieve that outcome. That's not true, why did I leave out? And here's the big one, I said that on purpose, I'll repeat it, I, Jordan Belfort possessed every possible tactic, strategy, skill, connection, relationship, ability, every, I had it all. I was that guy, I knew everything one would need to know to get where I wanted to go. And there's something missing from that equation, one know what it is, I'll tell you. Step number two, as part of this self belief, I knew that I could go out there and learn or attract into my life anything that was missing. I wasn't delusional to think I really knew everything I would need to know, but I knew for sure that whatever I didn't know, whatever I needed to learn, whoever I needed to attract, whatever had to happen, that I would figure it out, learn it, do it, accomplish it along the way. I had no doubt, no seeds of doubt, not even a shred of doubt, that there was nothing that I could possibly learn. And those first two steps, my friends, is the perfect storm for living an empowered life. It all starts there. You have the vision, but do you really believe that? Are you capable of it? Are you, do you already possess what you need to possess and footnote number two and whatever you don't possess, you know you can learn or do you really, at your level, you can figure deepest most honest self, say, you know, I'm really not that guy. I couldn't imagine myself being played by Leonor de Caprio or standing on a stage talking to 10,000 people, which I now do in my sleep. I do it so often. I don't believe I could run a billion dollar company. I don't believe I can come back from failure and lead people all over. I don't believe, I don't see myself as a beacon in light. I don't know enough about internet marketing right now. Yeah, I'm a great sales person, but I don't know about marketing. I don't, you know, this whole coding thing, you know, technology. I was born too early. I missed the boat in that. I don't know how to code. I would need to code. Or do you think that I can hire the best because my vision will be so inspiring that everyone's going to want to jump on board to make my vision a reality. And that now, my friend, takes us to step three is do you have the ability to share this vision in a way that connects with people, inspires them and moves them to take action and make them to help you accomplish it? Do you know how to share it? Do you really know? Could you look a top level programmer coder in the eye, explain what you want to accomplish and just sell say, wow, could you sit in front of a venture capitalist and talk about with passion and conviction and the authority financially and a deep tacit understanding of what you really do for a living? Meaning is someone going to give you a million bucks? Is someone going to pot with their hard earned money or harder money they manage other people? Is that you? Or is that not you? You're going to run into that situation on the way up to the top. And let me tell you something. You're going to want to be a top producer here. You want to be a number one guy in a company be valued and move up the chain, get big commission checks. And by the way, just you know, sales is the one job you don't have to own your own business to make tons of money. It's the one exemption to being, I think I'd be rich if you're not from normal, but if you're a commissioned based salesperson, you can make your business for yourself, my friend. You are. You got to reframe that limiting belief. You don't realize that the second you realize you're in business for yourself, you're not working for someone else. You're in a partnership with someone who's giving you the opportunity now to be a business owner working in a company as a salesman, as a commission based salesperson. You make as much as you want. Seriously. There are a lot of great companies, by the way, that are on the system now. They're coming like crazy, signing up like crazy. But if you're individually, you work for a company, if they're not on it, let them know. I'm happy to give you a finders fee for bringing in the company you work for. Happy to do that, to work something out. Everybody needs to be on the system. But do you feel comfortable saying it like I just said it, but I said to you, I'm like, you'd be crazy if you work in a company right now and you're on the system and your company's not. Why would you not go to your boss or say, buddy, you got to check this out. Just check it out. It's the greatest thing in the world because it is. Do you have the conviction and the where it will to look at everyone like I'm doing right now? I'm not shy or embarrassed about it. I believe I know how hard I've worked. That's the straight line is I want you. I insist that when you're done with this program and it never ends, it's about constant improvement. The core modules that it goes on and on, we reinforce, we learn, we grow, right? You have to be confident enough to ask people that you meet in the street day to day. How can I help you? And one of the things he brought up was just that's why I said it. I'm going to credit him because he said it. I am not doing things right. See, your vision is too far in the future. You can't connect up all the dots from where you are right now to the vision. That's too far. So we set these little goals in the middle that's, okay, I can see between now and four hours from now. And based on my results, I can decide, am I heading in the right direction or the wrong direction? And I consistently and continually pivot and pivot and pivot until I get better and better results. And I slowly but surely, through this process of pivoting, working, pivoting, analog pivoting, I end up moving towards my vision. Pretty logical, right? Now the danger here, and before we start the Q and A, what I really want to reinforce here is that what you're going to see in the real world, in real business, in real life, in real sales, that if you have what's called tunnel vision, two eyes, firmly focused, locked on your vision, do whatever it takes to rescue for failure and happiness, rigidity, not going to live a very happy and power life. We must have what's called bifurcated focus, meaning one eye, bifurcated, one eye, focused firmly on your vision with laser guided precision, while the other eye scans the horizon. It's like a radar scanning the horizon for opportunities that come up along the way on the way to your vision. And then you integrate those ones that make sense into your vision. Your vision's a living, breathing, evolving organism. But here's the really the rub when you get down to it. How would you know what might make sense to focus on? How would you know if it should be integrated if you didn't have a target to aim for? How would you know what opportunities to take and whatnot? How would you know? Without having the vision, you don't know what opportunities you should try to integrate into it. So what happens is the opportunities don't reveal themselves as opportunities. They reveal themselves as noise, other things that compete with your vision, inconsistencies and conclusions, you're like, ah, it's noise. No, even problems. Because you don't know exactly what you're aiming for. When you know exactly what you're aiming for, all of a sudden you can see, wait, it alerts your brain. This is important. That might potentially have value. And you start testing and integrating things into your existing vision. It evolves and grows. Let me just tell you something. I have tried this always, personally. And I've asked this question to a lot of really smart people. And a lot of people are not that smart, but have learned the hard way. There is very little power more than the basic that you get from just logically thinking it out about saying, I want to work hard because I want to make money to buy a car. That's a logic based thing. I want to make money on a nice car. Power comes when you tap into your unconscious mind. The deepest recesses of what is truly most important to you. And I promise you it ain't money. It's what the money will allow someone else you love unconditionally or a cause that you believe in at the highest level is dear to your heart.
